findings.description: No candidate rules were extracted for this blob at all, despite five distinct tint/transparency specifications under the 'Tint Rules' heading. All source units are unclustered, resulting in total loss of brand_rule coverage for this section.

rules.text: Campaign headline 'D' and 'M': ≈40% tint of the headline color (Gold or Brand Blue colorway)
No other ad-hoc tints — use palette values or these specified tints only

findings.description: u_color_scheme_rules_2_0005 is a governance/compliance statement ('No other ad-hoc tints — use palette values or these specified tints only') establishing a closed-set constraint over all preceding tint rules, but no rule captures its governance facets (hardness=hard, polarity=negative/prohibitive, scope=global override).

Missed relation: u_0005 acts as a governing/restrictive rule over the four preceding specific tint values (u_0001-u_0004), forming a 'defines exceptions/closed set' relation. This hierarchical relation was not captured because no rules exist at all.

u_0001 specifies an approximate value ('≈40% tint') applying conditionally to two specific headline labels ('D' and 'M') with colorway scoping (Gold or Brand Blue) — this should be flagged as soft/approximate hardness and scoped narrowly to campaign headlines, distinct from the exact percentage callout/wave rules which are hard-valued.
